content:
[Posted July 23, 2026 by corbet]

I have just received the shocking news that Dan Williams, a longtime,
high-profile kernel developer, has passed away. I knew him primarily
through his long service on the Linux Foundation Technical Advisory Board;
he was always a strong, thoughtful, and intelligent presence. Dan will be
deeply missed.

There is a support
effort underway for Dan's family as they come to terms with this loss.

to post comments

Condolences

Posted Jul 23, 2026 19:15 UTC (Thu)
by jpeisach (subscriber, #181966)
[Link]

Too new to me to get to know him, but old enough to have definitely lived under his influence. Rest in peace.

I wouldn't be here without his patience.

Posted Jul 23, 2026 19:20 UTC (Thu)
by gmprice (subscriber, #167884)
[Link]

Celebration of Life at Plumbers

Posted Jul 23, 2026 20:24 UTC (Thu)
by l1k (subscriber, #112260)
[Link] (1 responses)

Let's please have a celebration of life at Plumbers to commemorate Dan's huge contribution to this community, as we did for Daniel Bristot de Oliveira two years ago!

Celebration of Life at Plumbers

Posted Jul 24, 2026 1:11 UTC (Fri)
by broonie (subscriber, #7078)
[Link]

Very much so, we already started planning that.

Rest in peace

Posted Jul 24, 2026 2:50 UTC (Fri)
by yehs1 (guest, #125149)
[Link]

It's truly shocking. I miss the past times of our communication and the great help he gave me.

No words

Posted Jul 24, 2026 3:27 UTC (Fri)
by glikely (subscriber, #39601)
[Link]

I just heard the news and I’m absolutely gutted. Dan was the best of us. Kind and thoughtful. Dedicated and intelligent. He was one of my favourite people to talk to when we had the opportunity. He will be greatly missed

Will be greatly missed

Posted Jul 24, 2026 4:12 UTC (Fri)
by vaibhav92 (subscriber, #94105)
[Link]

Shocking news. I had the privilege of interacting with Dan during his time maintaining NVDIMM subsystem and he consistently came across as a very helpful and having remarkable foresight on how to do things the right way. I learned a great deal during those interactions, largely due to his patience, generousness and helpful nature. He will be greatly missed

Such a loss

Posted Jul 24, 2026 5:14 UTC (Fri)
by olof (subscriber, #11729)
[Link]

This is devastating news. Dan was one of the most relentlessly positive people I've known, and he always brought others with him with his enthusiasm. He was a genuinely good human being, and I will truly miss him.
